videos.title: How Sendai’s small businesses rebuilt and prepared after the 2011 disaster | UNDRR
videos.description:
In 2011, Sendai city was struck by a devastating earthquake and tsunami, causing an estimated ¥214.7 billion (USD 1.43 billion) in damage to commerce and industry. Many businesses were forced to stop — and some never recovered.

But for others, the disaster became a turning point.

videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction

videos.title: How small businesses in Barbados stay open when disasters strike | UNDRR
videos.description:
When disaster strikes, some businesses close their doors — and never reopen.
Others are ready.

videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction

videos.title: How Barbados turned USD 300 million of debt into climate resilience | UNDRR
videos.description:
What if debt could help protect people from climate shocks?

Barbados found a way to swap USD 300 million of high-interest debt for climate resilience — freeing up public finance to invest in water management and flood protection before disasters strike.

videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction

videos.title: How Guatemala increased disaster risk funding by 113% | UNDRR
videos.description:
What gets tracked gets funded.

When Guatemala analysed how public money was spent on disaster risk and climate action, a clear picture emerged: prevention was underfunded — and opportunity was being missed.

videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction

videos.description:
In response to Turkiye’s increasing need to manage floods, a delegation from Türkiye visited Japan in October 2025 to learn from Japan’s experience, expertise, and good practices in operating their flood early warning systems combined with its infrastructure measures.

videos.description:
Floods are becoming more frequent and severe in Mali — and displaced families face the highest risks.

videos.title: Barbados shows how to reach older persons before disaster strikes | UNDRR
videos.description:
In Barbados, older persons are no longer left behind when disasters loom.

Through inclusive risk communication and community-led action, seniors are now receiving early warnings they can understand, trust, and act on — helping save lives and reduce disaster risk. 🌪️📢

videos.title: Why there is no such thing as a natural disaster | UNDRR
videos.description:
🌍 Earthquakes, storms, floods — we often call them “natural disasters.” But that’s not quite true.

Nature creates hazards.
Disasters happen when those hazards hit vulnerable people and systems.

videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction

videos.title: Why people forget volcanic risk on Réunion Island | UNDRR
videos.description:
🌋 Volcanoes don’t stop being dangerous just because they’ve been quiet.

On Réunion Island, volcanic eruptions are part of daily life. Since 1979, the Piton de la Fournaise volcano has erupted 84 times, most often safely contained within an uninhabited caldera.

But that familiarity can be deceptive
videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction
